concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Edge Weight
concepts.short_definition: A numerical value assigned to an edge, representing cost or distance.

concepts.name: Path
concepts.short_definition: A sequence of connected edges from one vertex to another.

concepts.name: Path Length
concepts.short_definition: The sum of weights of edges in a path.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: A path between two vertices with the minimum possible path length.

concepts.name: Source Vertex
concepts.short_definition: The vertex from which paths originate.

concepts.name: Destination Vertex
concepts.short_definition: The vertex to which paths lead.

concepts.name: Distance
concepts.short_definition: The length of the shortest path from a source vertex to another vertex.

concepts.name: Initialization
concepts.short_definition: Setting initial values for distances and predecessors.

concepts.name: Relaxation
concepts.short_definition: Updating the distance to a vertex if a shorter path is found.

concepts.name: Iteration
concepts.short_definition: A complete cycle of processing all edges in the graph.

concepts.name: V-1 Iterations
concepts.short_definition: Performing the relaxation process for each vertex in the graph.

concepts.name: Negative Edge Weight
concepts.short_definition: An edge with a weight less than zero.

concepts.name: Negative Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A cycle in a graph where the sum of edge weights is negative.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path that starts and ends at the same vertex.

concepts.name: Bellman-Ford Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm to find the shortest paths from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph, even with negative edge weights.

concepts.name: Predecessor
concepts.short_definition: The vertex that comes immediately before a given vertex on a shortest path.

concepts.name: Path Reconstruction
concepts.short_definition: Using predecessors to trace back and find the actual shortest path.

concepts.name: Detecting Negative Cycles
concepts.short_definition: Identifying if a graph contains a negative cycle reachable from the source.

concepts.name: Vth Iteration
concepts.short_definition: An additional iteration after V-1 iterations to detect negative cycles.

concepts.name: Unreachable Vertices
concepts.short_definition: Vertices that cannot be reached from the source vertex.

concepts.name: Infinite Distance
concepts.short_definition: A representation of unreachable vertices or initial distance values.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the algorithm's execution time as a function of input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the algorithm's memory usage as a function of input size.

concepts.name: Dynamic Programming
concepts.short_definition: An algorithmic technique that solves problems by breaking them down into subproblems.

concepts.name: Subproblem
concepts.short_definition: A smaller, simpler version of the original problem.

concepts.name: Optimal Substructure
concepts.short_definition: The optimal solution to a problem contains optimal solutions to its subproblems.

concepts.name: Overlapping Subproblems
concepts.short_definition: Subproblems that are encountered multiple times during the computation.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: How a graph is stored in computer memory.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: Representing a graph using lists of adjacent vertices for each vertex.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: Representing a graph using a matrix where entries indicate edge presence.

concepts.name: Single Source Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: Finding shortest paths from one source to all other vertices.

concepts.name: All Pairs Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: Finding shortest paths between all pairs of vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for finding shortest paths in graphs with non-negative edge weights.

concepts.name: Comparison with Dijkstra
concepts.short_definition: Understanding the differences and use cases between Bellman-Ford and Dijkstra's algorithm.

concepts.name: Applications of Bellman-Ford
concepts.short_definition: Real-world problems where the Bellman-Ford algorithm is applied.

concepts.name: Routing Protocols
concepts.short_definition: Algorithms used in computer networks to determine paths for data packets.

concepts.name: Shortest Path Faster Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An optimization of the Bellman-Ford algorithm.
